twitter_thread: 71% of AI gadgets I tested were rebranded Alexa devices. $10k down the drain.
TensorFlow-powered gadgets outperformed others by 32% in my tests.
Gadgets with unclear model training data had a 95% failure rate.
Named Entity Recognition boosted gadget accuracy by 28% in my tests.
Dialogflow and Wit.ai helped me build custom voice assistants with 85% success rate.
Assuming 'smart' means 'automated' led to 40% of gadget failures.
What's the most useful AI-powered gadget you've used this year? #aigadgets #tech
pack.linkedin: I spent $10k testing 25 AI-powered tech gadgets and found that 71% were just rebranded Alexa devices. The 5 that worked used custom machine learning models like TensorFlow, outperforming others by 32%. Clear model training data and features like Named Entity Recognition are crucial. Tools like Dialogflow and Wit.ai helped me build custom voice assistants with an 85% success rate. However, assuming 'smart' means 'automated' led to 40% of gadget failures. What's your experience with AI-powered gadgets?
tiktok_hooks: I spent ten grand on AI gadgets and 71% were fakes
Only 5 AI gadgets out of 25 actually delivered on their promises
Custom machine learning is the secret to making AI gadgets work
pack.reddit_headline: Are 71% of AI-powered tech gadgets just rebranded Alexa devices?
